public_title: A prospective study evaluating quality of life and functional outcomes in patients with oral cancer undergoing free flap reconstruction at a tertiary cancer centre
scientific_title: Prospective cohort study of health-related quality of life, swallowing function, and speech outcomes following free flap reconstruction for oral cavity cancer: 12-month follow-up
health_condition: Oral cavity cancer requiring free flap reconstruction

intervention.name: Free flap reconstruction following oral cavity cancer resection (radial forearm, anterolateral thigh, or fibula flap)
intervention.details: No experimental intervention; observational follow-up of patients undergoing standard-of-care free flap reconstruction; outcomes measured at baseline, 3, 6, and 12 months

inclusion_criteria: Histologically confirmed squamous cell carcinoma of the oral cavity; Stage II, III, or IVA disease; Planned free flap reconstruction as part of curative surgical management; Age 18 years or older; ECOG performance status 0 or 1; Able to complete quality of life questionnaires in English, Hindi, or Tamil; Signed informed consent
exclusion_criteria: Stage IVB or IVC (metastatic) disease; Prior head and neck surgery or radiotherapy; Requirement of total glossectomy (too extensive for functional assessment); Cognitive impairment precluding QoL questionnaire completion; Life expectancy less than 12 months from non-cancer causes; Participation in concurrent interventional trial
